steps.title: Connect risk and reaction
steps.content: Theaters in Portland are described as favoring audience satisfaction over risk. An established classic is safer, while a work by a relatively unknown playwright would be riskier. The blank must describe reluctance toward that risky option.

steps.title: Reject mismatched attitudes
steps.content: Choice A means firmly committed, which is the opposite of risk avoidance. Choice C means lucky, and Choice D means caught off guard; neither describes a theater's cautious stance toward staging unknown work. The answer is B.
